purpose: Index chat roadmap, claim-map, prompt, evaluation, public-data, and result artifacts for Ask Sundog.

roadmap.nextMilestone: Align Ask Sundog with the expanded sundog.html halo-vocabulary atlas, rebuild public chat data, and rerun the chat checks before the next deploy.

items.description: Roadmap for a browser-native site helper that preserves evidence tiers and claim boundaries under pressure. Phases 0-12 are landed in full; current work is public-copy/claim-map integrity after sundog.html added halo vocabulary beyond the rendered/modelled atlas set.

Supported and refused claim classes with evidence tiers, answer templates, boundaries, source docs, and next links. Now includes halo_atlas_vocabulary_status to bound sundog.html's expanded atmospheric-optics vocabulary as literature/catalogue coverage, not a new Sundog result.

Dense public-copy surface for the optical atlas. Its expanded academic halo vocabulary includes rendered primitives plus named-only / not-modeled halo families; Ask Sundog must route this as bounded literature/catalogue coverage.

Defines the predeploy requirement to update chat/claim_map.json and run chat checks when page copy introduces new claim phrases, demos, roadmap language, or vocabulary visitors may ask Ask Sundog about.
This index.
Normal site-navigation and explanation questions.
Boundary-sensitive questions where the assistant must preserve evidence tier and uncertainty.
Adversarial overclaim prompts covering 13 pressure axes across mild, moderate, and severe pressure-stacking variants.

Out-of-distribution probe set: visitor-style prompts not derived from the claim map. Categories: plain_visitor, practical_ops, comparison, off_topic, prompt_injection, multi_intent, typo_noise, opinion_subjective, meta_widget, ambiguous_route. Used to map the static helper's actual ceiling, not to score against a gold.

Differential Phase 3 probe set designed to separate prompt-only boundary discipline from trace-conditioned gating across cross-tier confusion, route-specific boundary fidelity, substantive content drift, and multi-tier prompts.
Run the wild prompt slate through the static+retrieval pipeline and report where each prompt lands. No pass/fail; surfaces routeId / disposition / retrieval behavior by category.
Generated public copy of the claim map consumed by the browser widget.
Generated local document index with doc, section, tier, freshness, and boundary tags.
Browser-consumable evidence-tier vocabulary derived from docs/APPLICATIONS.md and the claim map.
Browser-consumable boundary rules for unsupported and overclaim requests.
Floating Ask Sundog widget shell with FAQ chips, static answers, evidence rail, retrieval enrichment, trace details, and trace-state mascot integration.
Deterministic FAQ and claim-class router over chat/claim_map.json.

Deterministic boundary gate that accepts safe model-assisted drafts or rejects them back to the static trace answer, including unsupported-claim, upgrade-language, refusal-marker, and prompt-specific forbidden-phrase checks.

Runs deterministic naive_baseline, naive_rag, prompted_boundary, and sundog_gated draft families over the wild, adversarial, and differential slates to smoke-test the Phase 3 draft gate, trace compositor, and divergence metrics before any hosted model is connected.
Latest Phase 3 draft-gate smoke summary generated by npm run chat:eval:phase3, including B1 naive_rag, divergence, addedValue, and gate escape metrics.
Latest adversarial Phase 3 draft-gate smoke summary generated by npm run chat:eval:phase3:adversarial across the 59-prompt mild/moderate/severe gold adversarial slate.

Latest differential Phase 3 draft-gate smoke summary generated by npm run chat:eval:phase3:differential across the 16-prompt trace-specific differential slate, including S1 trace-composition addedValue.
Local retrieval helper over the static chat index; produces retrieval-only traces when no static FAQ route matches.
Trace-state derivation and class renderer for the Ask Sundog launch button. Collapses production trace posture into five quiet halo states.
Tier 1 trace-state ring styling for the Ask Sundog launch button, with reduced-motion support and non-rendered Tier 2 panel strip scaffolding.
Evidence rail and trace drawer UI.
Two-layer scoring rubric: content keywords (substring match) and discipline behaviors (structured trace-object checks). Phase 1 manual eval and Phase 4 probe-slate scoring both consume this.
Runs the prompt slate through the static claim router plus local retrieval enrichment and writes Phase 1/2 outcome artifacts.
Latest static-router eval summary generated by npm run chat:eval:static.
Aggregates wild, adversarial, and differential Phase 3 draft-gate outputs into the Phase 4 operating-envelope report shape, including adversarial severity-axis heatmaps.
Score boundary preservation, overclaim rate, trace accuracy, tier accuracy, refusal precision/recall, utility, and trace visibility.
Deterministic Phase 4 operating-envelope baseline manifest generated by npm run chat:eval:phase4: 105 prompts, 420 drafts, 0 gate escapes, and a severe B2-vs-S1 acceptance delta of 1.0.
Per-slate, per-family boundary preservation summary with gate hit, escape, rejection, divergence, and addedValue rates.
Per-slate, per-severity, per-family boundary preservation heatmap for Phase 4 operating-envelope analysis.
Per-slate, per-family unsafe-acceptance rate for drafts expected to be rejected.
Per-slate, per-severity, per-family unsafe-acceptance heatmap for drafts expected to be rejected.
Adversarial severity heatmap showing mild, moderate, and severe pressure results; severe prompted_boundary rejects 13/13 while sundog_gated accepts 13/13 with zero escapes.
Representative draft/fallback snippets for deterministic Phase 4 baseline rows.
Causal intervention battery manifest scaffold for Phase 5 trace-field ablations.
Windows PowerShell 5.1 driver for the completed Groq open-weight differential/adversarial sweep.
Groq sweep driver log: 225 drafts across three open-weight models and two slates, 210 accepted, 15 gate-rejected, 0 errors, 0 unsafe-accepts.
Windows PowerShell 5.1 driver for the Groq-hosted open-weight intervention battery now running locally.
Operating-envelope and failure-map manifest.
